title: Fix with_timeout NIF contract mismatch
purpose: The Gleam SDK passes a closure to with_timeout, but the Rust NIF (nif_timer.rs) decodes args[1] as a string. Both sides pass their own tests because each tests against its own fake. This is the clearest example of the integration-seam gap pattern.
task: Align the NIF implementation with the Gleam SDK contract so that with_timeout correctly executes the operation closure with a deadline, returning the operation result or a timeout error. Use beamr's continuation trampoline pattern (ProcessContext::set_continuation_trampoline) to call the closure — NIFs cannot re-enter the interpreter directly. Reference maps_bifs.rs (bif_maps_fold) in beamr for the canonical pattern. The NIF sets up a trampoline to call the closure, returns Ok(Term::NIL), and the scheduler calls the closure; the continuation's resume function receives the result and applies timeout logic. Multi-step continuations can be chained via ContinuationStep::Call. A NativeContinuation variant must be added for Aion's timeout continuation.

requirements.title: NIF accepts closure
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL decode the operation argument as a callable (Gleam function reference), not a string, matching the aion_flow SDK's with_timeout(operation: fn() -> Result(a, e), timeout_ms: Int) signature.
acceptance: The with_timeout NIF decodes args[1] as a callable function reference.
The NIF argument types match the aion_flow SDK's published with_timeout signature.
Passing a non-callable value returns a clear argument-type error.

requirements.title: Operation execution with deadline
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL use beamr's continuation trampoline (ProcessContext::set_continuation_trampoline) to execute the operation closure, then apply timeout logic in the continuation's resume function. The NIF returns Ok(Term::NIL) and the scheduler calls the closure; the resume function races the result against a durable timer.
acceptance: The operation closure is executed via the continuation trampoline pattern, not by re-entering the interpreter from the NIF.
A NativeContinuation variant is registered for the with_timeout continuation.
The continuation's resume function receives the closure result and applies timeout logic.
If the operation completes before the timeout, its result is returned.
If the timeout fires before the operation completes, a timeout error is returned.
The timeout duration is taken from args[0] as an integer milliseconds value.

requirements.title: Replay compatibility
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL record the with_timeout outcome as a durability event so that replay returns the recorded result without re-executing the operation or re-waiting.
acceptance: The with_timeout outcome (success or timeout) is recorded as a durability event through the Recorder.
On replay, with_timeout returns the recorded result without calling the operation closure.
On replay, with_timeout returns the recorded result without waiting for the timeout duration.
The recorded event distinguishes between operation-completed and timed-out outcomes.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the aion_flow SDK's public with_timeout signature.
SHALL NOT introduce wall-clock time into the determinism boundary.
verification: cargo test passes for the timer NIF module.
gleam build succeeds for a workflow using with_timeout.
Integration test demonstrates timeout firing and operation completing before timeout.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
